topic: Someone receives a package intended for the previous tenant
raw_output:
[story 1]

The knock on the door sounded like an unwelcome intrusion. I straightened up from the sofa, a low groan escaping my lips before I even reached for the door handle. Another delivery. I peered through the peephole, and my jaw tightened. It was a box, clearly addressed to the previous occupants, sitting squarely on my doorstep. I sighed, a long, drawn-out sound that seemed to deflate some of my already frayed patience. I picked up the package with two fingers, the cardboard feeling cheap and unwelcome in my hand. Who sends things to the wrong place? I just wanted it gone.

[story 2]

I stared at the delivery notification on my phone, the notification flashing a reminder of this unwelcome item. A sharp spike of annoyance shot through me, making my shoulders tense beneath my sweater. I walked to the entryway, my steps deliberate and heavy. There it was, sitting on the welcome mat—a package clearly meant for someone else. I nudged it with my toe, a gesture of profound displeasure. I took a deep breath, trying to keep my expression neutral, but the slight clenching in my jaw betrayed me. I had to deal with this.

[story 3]

When the courier handed over the parcel, I didn't bother to look at them properly. My eyes flicked from the box to the pavement, a clear sign of my displeasure radiating off me. It was another piece of unsolicited mail, another thing cluttering up my space. I snatched the package, the paper crinkling loudly under my grip. I dropped it heavily onto the floor near the door, the thud echoing in the quiet hallway. I just wanted the whole ordeal to conclude so I could go back to ignoring the world.
